zh.Hatfield, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾哈特菲爾德
zh.lifeSummary: William Smythe於大約1494年出生在英國越可爾哈特菲爾德。他於1518年與Catherine在英國越可爾坎特利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於從1559年1月25日到1560年5月2日在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國越可爾哈特菲爾德。

zh-hans.Hatfield, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾哈特菲爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Smythe于大约1494年出生在英国越可爾哈特菲爾德。他于1518年与Catherine在英国越可爾坎特利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于从1559年1月25日到1560年5月2日在他的家乡，埋葬在英国越可爾哈特菲爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: William Smythe was born about 1494, in Hatfield, Yorkshire, England. He married Catherine in 1518, in Cantley, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died from 25 January 1559 to 2 May 1560, in his hometown, and was buried in Hatfield, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: William Smythe est né environ 1494, à Hatfield, Everwyck, Angleterre. Il a épousé Catherine en 1518, à Cantley, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé du 25 janvier 1559 au 2 mai 1560, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Hatfield, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: William Smythe wurde um 1494 in Hatfield, Yorkshire, England geboren. Er hat 1518 in Cantley, Yorkshire, England Catherine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb von 25. Januar 1559 bis 2. Mai 1560 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Hatfield, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: William Smythe è nato il circa 1494 nel Hatfield, Yorkshire, England. Lui sposato Catherine nel 1518, nel Cantley, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì da 25 gennaio 1559 a 2 maggio 1560, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Hatfield, Yorkshire, England.

ja.Hatfield, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアハトフィールド
ja.Cantley, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアカントリー
ja.lifeSummary: William Smytheは約1494年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハトフィールドで生まれた。1518年にイギリスヨークシュアイアカントリーでCatherineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼はから1559年1月25日まで1560年5月2日に故郷で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアハトフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Smythe(은)는 대략 1494년 영국 요르크실르 하트필드에서 태어났다. 그는 1518년 영국 요르크실르 캔틀리에서 Catherine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1559년 1월 25일부터 1560년 5월 2일까지 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 하트필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Smythe nasceu aproximadamente 1494, em Hatfield, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Catherine em 1518, em Cantley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu de 25 de janeiro de 1559 a 2 de maio de 1560, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Hatfield, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: William Smythe родился около 1494 г. в местности хатфиелд, еркшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine в 1518 г. в местности кантли, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер с 25 января 1559 по 2 мая 1560 в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности хатфиелд, еркшир, Англия.
